backlog.title: Spike: MCP SDK + two-layer pattern
backlog.description: Prove the two-layer MCP architecture in practice: an orchestrator MCP server that coordinates a downstream agent MCP server. Measure latency. Evaluate SDK options. Produce a GO/NO-GO verdict.
acceptance_criteria: spikes/mcp-two-layer/ contains a working proof of concept
PoC demonstrates: external client → orchestrator MCP → agent MCP → result
Latency measured for a representative multi-step task
SDK options evaluated (Python MCP SDK, TypeScript MCP SDK, others)
decisions/ADR-001-mcp-two-layer.md written with GO/NO-GO verdict and rationale

backlog.title: Spike: kagent evaluation
backlog.description: Evaluate kagent as Lume's agent runtime. Can it run Lume's agents as CRDs on k3s? What does Lume need to add? Produce a GO/NO-GO verdict.
acceptance_criteria: spikes/kagent/ contains evaluation notes and any PoC code
Tested: deploying a simple kagent CRD on k3s inside devcontainer
Evaluated: what kagent provides vs what Lume needs beyond it
Evaluated: MCP tool integration with kagent agents
decisions/ADR-002-kagent.md written with GO/NO-GO verdict and rationale

backlog.title: Spike: GitOps operator + Crossplane on k3s
backlog.description: Evaluate Flux vs Argo CD for GitOps reconciliation. Prove Crossplane provider-kubernetes for local profile on k3s. Produce a decision + ADR.
acceptance_criteria: spikes/gitops/ contains evaluation notes and any PoC config
Flux and Argo CD both stood up on k3s and compared on: complexity, resource use, Lume fit
Crossplane provider-kubernetes deployed on k3s and a test claim reconciled
decisions/ADR-003-gitops-operator.md written with Flux vs Argo decision and rationale
decisions/ADR-004-crossplane.md written with GO/NO-GO for Crossplane approach

backlog.title: Spike: local model evaluation
backlog.description: Measure actual task performance of candidate local models for each role in Lume's model routing table. Produce documented model assignments with performance data.
acceptance_criteria: spikes/local-models/ contains benchmarks and test cases
Models tested: llama3.2:3b (router), qwen2.5-coder:7b (code), qwen2.5-coder:14b (complex code), at minimum
Each model measured on representative tasks for its assigned role
Pass/fail rate and latency documented for each role
decisions/ADR-005-model-routing.md written with final model assignments and evidence

backlog.title: Spike: validation loop proof of concept
backlog.description: Build a standalone validation loop PoC (not inside Lume): PLAN → ACT → VALIDATE → REFINE → ESCALATE. Prove that done/failures/next_action are deterministic outputs, never inferred.
acceptance_criteria: spikes/validation-loop/ contains a working standalone PoC
PoC demonstrates all 5 loop stages with a real (if simple) task
VALIDATE output is a typed struct: done: bool, failures: list, next_action: str
LLM is used only in PLAN and REFINE stages — VALIDATE is fully deterministic
Escalation chain tested: local failure → larger local → cloud
decisions/ADR-006-validation-loop.md written with conclusions

backlog.title: Design provisioning agent boundary
backlog.description: Document the provisioning agent's tool set, bootstrap flow, and config structure. Differentiate clearly from the infra agent.
acceptance_criteria: docs/provisioning-agent.md exists
All MCP tools defined: name, description, inputs, outputs, git-write behaviour
bootstrap_lume_project flow documented end-to-end
Config structure documented: what a provisioned project's repo contains
Clear boundary with Infra Agent: no overlapping responsibilities
Neil has reviewed and approved (explicit APPROVE in conversation)

backlog.title: Design project template
backlog.description: Define what every Lume-provisioned project gets: directory structure, CLAUDE.md, hedl setup, CI config, devcontainer config, etc.
acceptance_criteria: docs/project-template.md exists
Template structure documented with rationale for each element
CLAUDE.md template content defined
hedl configuration for new projects defined
Dogfooding loop documented: provisioned projects are built like Lume

backlog.title: Write high-level plan (phases 1-3)
backlog.description: Synthesise all Phase 0 outputs into a high-level plan for phases 1-3: draft DoD, known unknowns, risks, and build order rationale.
acceptance_criteria: docs/plan.md exists
Phases 1, 2, 3 each have: goal, draft DoD (binary criteria), known unknowns, risks
Build order rationale: why this sequence, what unblocks what
Phase 0 findings referenced: each spike conclusion influences the plan
Neil has reviewed and approved (explicit APPROVE in conversation)

backlog.title: Reconcile CLAUDE.md to approved requirements
backlog.description: Update CLAUDE.md so its framing matches the approved docs/requirements.md and design docs. Reconcile named contradictions: (1) security-model 'High always requires human approval — no exceptions' vs the earned-autonomy posture; (2) principle 10 'OSS only — no paid runtime dependencies' vs operator-opt-in paid services (Bedrock, Claude Pro); (3) the IDP-only vision framing vs platform+capabilities+templates; (4) Infra's 'scale, debug, observe' vs the WORK-0003 decision that 'observe' belongs to Obs (signal vs action); (5) the provisional 'write_gitops_config' tool vs its WORK-0003 split by artifact class (Infra write_workload_manifest / Provisioning write_crossplane_claim); (6) the flat '5 agents' framing vs Work being a deterministic service (no inference). Raised by adversarial review of WORK-0001 and WORK-0003.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md vision framing matches docs/requirements.md (platform + capabilities + templates; SDLC is first template)

CLAUDE.md security model reconciled with the earned-autonomy posture (no silent contradiction on high-blast-radius approval); the demotion of the general high-blast 'no exceptions' to a default MUST preserve the permanent core-touching 'no exceptions' (WORK-0006 §4)
CLAUDE.md principle 10 (OSS only) reconciled with operator-opt-in paid services
CLAUDE.md agent descriptions reconciled with docs/agent-boundaries.md (observe is Obs's; write_gitops_config split by artifact class; Work is a deterministic service)
No requirement from docs/requirements.md or the approved design docs is contradicted by CLAUDE.md after the edit
Neil has reviewed and approved (explicit APPROVE in conversation)

Approval gate requirements: which actions require human approval and why
Rollback requirements: what can be rolled back, how, within what window
Self-modification safety: constraints on Lume modifying its own code or config

completed.title: Write security and auth requirements
completed.description: Document the threat model, identity model, blast radius controls, audit requirements, and build-vs-adopt decisions for Lume's security architecture.
acceptance_criteria: docs/security-requirements.md exists

Identity model: all identity levels and their trust/permission scope
Blast radius controls: how each level is enforced, who can approve high-blast actions
Audit requirements: what is logged, where, retention, tamper-evidence
Build vs adopt: for each security capability, decision on building or adopting existing tool
